name: ETH 4h Exhaustion Reacceptance Rotation v1
edge_mechanism: a directional stretch fails, prior value is reaccepted, and price rotates back toward medium balance in a chop-dominant market
thesis: Cycle 72 includes mean reversion from exhaustion only when structure reaccepts prior value. ETH 4h is the best-fit lane because it remains 100% chop and earlier continuation families there have either bled or died. That makes value reacceptance the structurally correct side of the market. The edge is not fading strength blindly. It is acting only after value has been reclaimed.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h reacceptance events still undertrade or fail to produce enough movement back toward balance after costs, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price stretches away from value, cannot sustain the move, reclaims value, and rotates back toward medium balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In pure chop, failed excursions should be more common than durable breakouts. Reacceptance after the failed excursion is the cleanest structural proof that the market is returning to balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because it is the clearest pure-chop lane in the basket and should support value-based mean reversion better than trend-up TAO.

indicator_role_map.EMA_55: medium balance destination and structure anchor

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Real continuation bursts may still get faded if the regime temporarily escapes chop.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets work but overall expectancy remains thin, tighten the time exit before adding more entry filters.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Frequent modest balance rotations, many first-target captures, low dependence on huge runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h clears density and produces materially better participation than the recent sparse continuation families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated reacceptance entries that instantly re-break the stretched side.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low relative to recent dead continuation concepts

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit pure-chop lane for exhaustion reacceptance rotation.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough reacceptance rotations with positive first-target expectancy.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h stays too sparse or the moves are too shallow after costs.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate-to-high participation with many modest value rotations.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same exhaustion/reacceptance logic survives a weaker, more erratic lane.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h is too unstable and destroys the value-reacceptance structure.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count, larger snap-back moves than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Negative control to measure how much this mean-reversion thesis depends on chop.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h still shows failed excursions snapping back to value cleanly.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the idea is too anti-trend there.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger mean-reversion rotations than ETH.

long: Exit remaining 20% on touch of EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on touch of EMA_55 or 4 bars
